Mercury's children named Taskara (Thieves), numbering
61 are faint, slender, long and white. They may appear in any
direction and are harmful in effect.
[Gf. ™—

On Comets XI 129
tyfe i
7TF?Tr ^ ^Crr5TmfemT: .'?I^T: n^?u
The sons of Mars are 60 in number and are called Kauhkuma.
They resemble blood and fire, are possessed of three crests as well
as three stars each, and appear in the north, They are productive
of disastrous effects.
[Cf. ™—
sTnT^RWixTTfTT^rt ^ n
t?TT ^r: trfg": i
ii]
^TcIT: I
There are 33 comets well known as Tamasakilakas (Dark
Shafts) that are the children of Rihu. They are seen on the discs
of the Sun and the Moon. Their effects have been already des-
cribed in the chapter on the 'Sun's Transit'.
[These Dark shafts might be the prototypes of the modern
solar flares and sun-spots.
